overview: Agent Board is a VS Code extension for coordinating AI coding agents through repo-native task files, project context, and workflow instructions.
goals: Make task state visible in VS Code.
Let Codex and Claude Code claim, build, update, QA, and complete tasks through files.
Avoid copy-pasted prompts by storing durable task and project context in the repository.
architectureNotes: The extension backend uses the VS Code Extension API and local JSON storage in .agent-board/. The board UI is a React webview bundled with esbuild.
codingRules: Follow existing TypeScript and React patterns in this repository.
Keep extension backend logic in src/ and webview UI logic in webview/.
Preserve unknown fields in task and project JSON files.
Avoid real Claude or Codex API calls until explicitly added as a later integration.
agentRules: Read .agent-board/project.json before claiming or implementing a task.
Use the task JSON as the task source of truth.
Update relevantFiles, agentNotes, activityLog, and lastUpdated as work progresses.
Run validation commands before moving a task to ready-for-qa.
designRules: The board should feel like an operational command centre for coding agents.
Prefer dense, scannable controls over marketing-style layout.
Keep task controls obvious and reduce repeated manual clicks.
glossary: Agent Board: the VS Code visual board.
.agent-board/: repo-native source of truth for agents.
Agent Spec: generated task description, acceptance criteria, QA checklist, and constraints.
